title: Completed the corrected GOAT strategy: hunch status (activation) + cross-client grounding propagation (portability) — v0.37.0
context: Final two of the four corrected-strategy priorities (capture quality + benchmark shipped in 0.36.2/0.36.3; activation + portability here).
decision: ACTIVATION (#2 advisory-first trust): added `hunch status` — an enforcement-readiness dashboard showing firmness (+ plain-English meaning), ARMED confirmed blocking invariants split precise-vs-scope-only, drafts to confirm (ready/scrutiny via partitionReview), stale rules (store.staleness), and a nudge when precise rules are armed but firmness isn't strict. Reuses existing helpers; no new infra. PORTABILITY (#3): a recorded rule now propagates to EVERY assistant's grounding, not just CLAUDE.md — root cause was that record-constraint / review --accept / --accept-verified / the record-removal path only called updateClaudeMd (CLAUDE.md), leaving AGENTS.md / Copilot / Cursor / Windsurf stale until the next index/sync. Swapped those four call sites to refreshExistingGrounding (all five docs, refresh-only), and added the same to the hunch_record_correction MCP path (public-only — a private rule must never render into committed grounding).
consequences: A rule taught once (CLI or a correction via any assistant's MCP) is now held by every assistant pointed at the repo — the cross-client portability the GOAT thesis named, for the GROUNDING surface (the deterministic guard was already client-agnostic at commit/CI). True edit-time DENY in non-Claude clients remains vendor-blocked (no equivalent hook API) — unchanged.
`hunch status` closes the activation gap (the moat sat dormant until you remembered to confirm/set strict): one screen tells you what's enforcing and the exact next action.
Refresh-only + public-only by construction: Claude-only projects are unchanged; private rules never leak into committed grounding (con_e04226bd05 client-agnostic invariant preserved).

Verified end-to-end: a rule recorded once lands in all 5 grounding docs; status shows the armed rule + firmness nudge. test/selfheal.test.ts +1 (multi-client propagation); full suite 355 pass; shipped to npm (0.37.0).
All four corrected-strategy priorities are now implemented: capture quality (0.36.2), benchmark honesty (0.36.3), activation + portability (0.37.0).
